Off Calendar Jul-31-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Milla Lvovich appearing via zoom for and with Gwendolyn Sacco (petitioner).
Carolyn D. Cain appearing via zoom for Creditor Joscelyn Jones.
Robert Cowan appearing via zoom for beneficiaries Paula Bessard, Luzell Sander, Jasmine Coussaint and Cynthia Estrada.

Discussion was held regarding the estate having no assets and only a 1971 vehicle. 1. Final report of executor; 2. Petition for allowance of statutory compensation to executor and her attorney; 3. Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Chavez. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jul-25-2022 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jun-27-2022 Continued To Jul-25-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Milla Lvovich appeared by zoom for Gwendolyn Sacco.

The court noted that this is the fourth appearance on this status hearing, that the parties had been previously ordered four different times to file a petition, that it looks like it was finally done one court day ago, that because of the very late filing the court has not had time to review it and questions why it took so long to get this filed. Ms. Lvovich apologized to the court, stated that they did their best to get the petition filed, but were unable to, that her client had deaths in her family and sickness in some family members for which she needed to care for, and that everything was submitted on Friday. The court noted that similar facts had been provided at prior hearings as far back as February. Ms. Lvovich explained that on May 2nd she did not know if they were going to file a petition to close the estate, or a petition to keep the estate open, that they finalized a distribution agreement in the trust that went out to all of the beneficiaries, and that based on the agreement they were able to file a petition for final distribution. Ms. Lvovich once again apologized to the court, and stated that she does not remember what the problem was back in October, that there had been a division between the beneficiaries of the trust, that it has been fairly complex, and that is why it has dragged on and has taken some time. Status on close of the estate is continued to July 25, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Lydia Villareal. Probate Examiner: Brandon Slykhous. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: S. Phan. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar May-02-2022 Continued To Jun-27-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Milla Lvovich, via Zoom, for Gwendolyn Sacco, Personal Representative.

The court reminds counsel of its request two months ago for her to file a petition before the calling of this matter which has not been done. Counsel offers testimony as to what caused the delay and asks for an additional continuance. Counsel reports that she believes a Petition for Final Distribution will be filed soon.

The Status on Close of Estate is continued to Jun-27-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. The court orders counsel to file either a Petition for Final Distribution or a Petition for Approval of Status of Administration one week prior to the next hearing date.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Andam. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Feb-16-2022 Continued To May-02-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Milla Lvovich, via ZOOM, for Gwendolyn Sacco, Personal Representative.

The court asks for an update regarding the estate. Counsel reports that the estate is insolvent, and that the lone asset of the estate is one real property of which the sale is pending. The court advises counsel that if the estate is not ready to close a Petition for Approval of Status of Administration will be required. Counsel requests a two-month continuance.

The Status on Close of Estate is continued to May-02-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: J. Jeung. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Oct-27-2021 Continued To Feb-16-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Milla Lvovich, via ZOOM, for Gwendolyn Sacco, Executor.

Counsel reports that the estate currently has no funds but that funds will be transferred to the estate as soon as a pending sale of real property is finalized. Counsel also advises that creditor's claims will be satisfied at that time.

The Status on Close of Estate is continued to Feb-16-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Jul-27-2020 Denied

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Aug-04-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Milla Lovovich appeared by zoom for and with Gwendolyn Sacco (Objector/Petitioner) by zoom.
Carolyn Cain appeared by zoom for Jocelyn Jones-Torru (Petitioner/Objector).
Virginia Brook appeared by zoom in Propria Persona.
Luzelle Sanders appeared by zoom in Propria Persona.
Paula Bessard appeared by zoom in Propria Persona.

Ms. Cain stated that Ms. Jones-Torru is a judgment creditor and has perfected the judgment by recording a lien, that Ms. Sacco should not be appointed, that Ms. Sacco is not the sole beneficiary, that Ms. Sacco has neglected both estates and that nothing has been done. Ms. Lovovich stated that her Client is appointed as the Executor under the will, that the only known asset is the real property which is in the trust and that her Client has been administering the trust. Ms. Lovovich also stated that her Client made an offer to pay Ms. Jones-Torru however, there are no liquid assets, that they informed Ms. Jones-Torru that she can only be paid once the house is sold, referred to Probate Code 8420, and stated that no creditor should be appointed over a named Executor and Child of the Decedent. Ms. Cain stated that the offer that was made was to reduce the amount of interest, that she believes there are assets in the estate and that if the Court is inclined to appoint Ms. Sacco, it holds her to the statutory guidelines. The family members expressed their concerns, spoke of the jewelry Ms. Bessard had, the cash that Ms. Bessard had, items being stolen from Ms. Bessard, the asbestos case involving Ms. Bessard's husband, the family dynamics, the level of care that was provided and by whom, etc. The Court denied Ms. Jones-Torru's Petition for Letters of Administration. The Court granted Ms. Sacco's Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary (subject to review of the order). The issue of whether or not bond will be required is to be addressed when the inventory and appraisal is filed. Status on close of estate is set for September 27, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status of the estate is set for October 26, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Court Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: C. Cabasares. Not reported.
